finding: On Apr 10, seven sessions were opened between 21:52 and 23:22 Stockholm time, all iterating on a date-night planner agent and Google Calendar integration. Three of these sessions started after 23:00. While the task is lightweight (agent setup, not deep coding), rapid iteration past bedtime can delay wind-down. This is a single-evening occurrence so severity is low, but it matches the manic-mode signature of multiple short sessions in rapid succession. The existing late-night-warn.py hook is already in place as mitigation.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py hook fires on UserPromptSubmit to warn about late-night work.
